game_name: Jordan vs. Bird: One on One

design_philosophy.core_loop: One-on-one basketball matchup: select player (Jordan or Bird), execute offensive moves and defensive positioning, score points through layups/dunks or three-pointers, accumulate wins
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Head-to-head competitive play between two iconic basketball players with asymmetrical abilities (Jordan's dunk contest vs Bird's three-point contest)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Michael Jordan or Larry Bird in intimate one-on-one basketball competition; mastering signature moves of real NBA athletes
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Stripped-down, minimalist approach to basketball simulation—only two players, no team mechanics, focus on individual skill execution and showmanship (minigames) rather than strategic team play; celebrates individual star power over team dynamics

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects which of two characters to play; character choice affects available minigame types (dunk contest vs three-point contest) and presumably playstyle

progression.unlock_mechanism: Minigames unlock based on player character selection (Jordan: slam dunk contest; Bird: three-point contest)
replayability_hooks: Two distinct playable characters with different minigame specializations
One-on-one competitive structure supports head-to-head multiplayer
Asymmetrical character abilities encourage replaying with opposite player

world_building_techniques: Character selection screen features real NBA players (Michael Jordan, Larry Bird)
Minigame theming ties to real player signature abilities (Jordan's dunking prowess, Bird's three-point shooting)

faction_indicators: Chicago Bulls vs. Boston Celtics allegiance through character selection
Michael Jordan brand vs. Larry Bird brand player identity
environmental_storytelling: Character selection implicitly signals sporting allegiance and era preference (1980s NBA rivalry)
Minigame specialization reflects real-world player mythologies
